title: salvia bogotensis subsp. sochensis
intro: Salvia bogotensis subsp. sochensis, also known as Colombian Sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia bogotensis plant, a member of the mint family native to Colombia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and soothing digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Salvia bogotensis subsp. sochensis typically involve infusions or teas made from the leaves, which can be consumed internally or used externally in the form of creams or ointments for skin issues.
